sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to fruits of commercial varieties of papayas grown from Carica papaya L., of the Caricaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.  Papayas for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to fruits of commercial varieties of papayas grown from Carica papaya L., of the Caricaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.  Papayas for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to fruits of commercial varieties of papayas grown from Carica papaya L., of the Caricaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.  Papayas for industrial processing are excluded.

Classification
sections.text: Papayas are classified in three classes defined below:

sections.title: The papayas must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness account being taken of the characteristics of the variety and/or commercial type and the area in which they are grown.
path_titles: The papayas must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness account being taken of the characteristics of the variety and/or commercial type and the area in which they are grown.
sections.text:
The development and condition of the papayas must be such as to enable them:
- to withstand transport and handling; and
- to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Papayas in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

number=2.2.1
level=3
Papayas in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Papayas in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text:
Papayas in this class must be of good quality.  They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:
- slight defects in shape;
- slight skin defects (i.e. mechanical bruising, sun spots and/or latex burns). The total area affected shall not exceed 10% of the total surface.
The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text:
Size is determined by the weight of the fruit with a minimum weight of 200 g, in accordance with the following table:
Size Code Weight (in grams)
A 200 – 300
B 301 – 400
C 401 – 500
D 501 – 600
E 601 – 700
F 701 – 800
G 801 – 1100
H 1101 – 1500
I 1501 – 2000
J > 2001

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package (or in each lot for produce presented in bulk) for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
number=4
level=1
Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package (or in each lot for produce presented in bulk) for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

page_spans.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package (or in each lot for produce presented in bulk) for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: This class includes papayas which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the papayas retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: - defects in shape; - defects in colouring; - skin defects (i.e., mechanical bruising, sun spots and latex burns). The total area affected should not exceed 15% of the total surface; - slight marks caused by pests. The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.text: For all classes, 10% by number or weight of papayas corresponding to the size immediately above and/or below that indicated on the package, with a minimum of 190 g for those papayas packed in the smallest size range.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
number=5
level=1

sections.title: MARKING OR LABELLING
sections.retrieval_text:
MARKING OR LABELLING
number=6
level=1

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be uniform and contain only papayas of the same origin, variety and/or commercial type, quality and size. For “Extra” Class, colour and ripeness should be uniform. The visible part of the contents of the package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be representative of the entire contents.

page_spans.text: The contents of each package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be uniform and contain only papayas of the same origin, variety and/or commercial type, quality and size. For “Extra” Class, colour and ripeness should be uniform. The visible part of the contents of the package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Papayas must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new2, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Papayas shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).

sections.title: Non-retail Containers
sections.text: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, or in the documents accompanying the shipment. For produce transported in bulk, these particulars must appear on a document accompanying the goods.

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the papayas. Packages (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sentences.original: Papayas in this class must be of superior quality.

classification.sentence: They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
classification.pattern: [Main Obligation] + [Exception introduced by 'with the exception of'] + [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.obligation: They must be free of defects
components.exception: with the exception of very slight superficial defects
components.condition: provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation to be defect-free but includes a specific exception and a condition qualifying that exception. This composite structure with both exception and conditional qualification does not strongly match any known templates, leading to a new pattern classification with high confidence in modality and function.
notes: The sentence combines an obligation with both an exception and a condition, which is a complex normative structure absent from the current template catalog.

notes: The sentence functions as a normative extension clause referencing another standard and adding obligations, which is distinct from typical obligation templates focused on direct action requirements.

sentences.original: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.

prefilter.reason: Relevant for regulatory context and contains specific references.

classification.sentence: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.
classification.pattern: Recommendation clause introduced by 'It is recommended that' followed by obligation to follow defined hygiene and practice codes.

components.recommendation: produce ... be prepared and handled in accordance with
components.references: General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts

classification.justification: Normative recommendation for handling; phrased as recommendation rather than direct obligation; no matching templates found.
notes: Typical recommendation form with references to standards.

sentences.original: The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

classification.sentence: The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
classification.pattern: Subject + modal verb (should) + verb (comply) + object (criteria) + relative clause specifying criteria source

components.subject: The produce
components.criteria_source: Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997)

classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation by requiring compliance with established microbiological criteria. Its structure features a subject followed by a modal verb and verb phrase describing compliance with specific criteria referenced by a guideline. No exact or close template from the catalog matches this specific compliance citation structure, resulting in a new pattern classification.
notes: The modal 'should' here conveys an obligation in normative regulatory context.
The sentence references an external standard for criteria but does not specify numerical limits, hence falls under obligation rather than limits and targets.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR PAPAYA
pages.summary: Página apresenta título do documento e cabeçalho, sem estrutura de seções ou texto explicativo. Nada para mapear em hierarquia nesta página.

Página mapeada contendo dois níveis principais: 'DEFINITION OF PRODUCE' como nível 1 e 'PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY' como nível 1 com subníveis 2.1 e 2.2 e seus respectivos subtítulos e textos associados. Estrutura clara de seções e subseções numeradas para uso em RAG.

pages.title: CXS 183-1993
pages.summary: A página contém três seções principais numeradas 2.2.3 (subseção de nível 2), 3, e 4, com a seção 4 detalhada em subseções e subsubseções numéricas hierarquicamente organizadas para uma estrutura clara para RAG. Texto associado está limpo e estruturado conforme os títulos com continuidade correta para a seção 2.2.3 da página anterior.

Página contém duas seções principais 5 e 6, cada uma com subseções e subsubseções detalhadas, estruturadas numericament e textos associados corretamente para RAG. Títulos aparecem claramente, sem indicações de continuidade entre páginas nesta página.

Página contém duas seções principais numeradas 7 e 8, cada uma com subseções numeradas 7.1, 7.2, 8.1 e 8.2 com textos relacionados de acordo com a estrutura. Não há indicações de continuidade entre páginas. Cabeçalho identificado como 'CXS 183-1993  5'. Documento não tem título visível nesta página.
